description: On the home page, the flight search form does not appear; the hero section where users enter origin, destination, date and passengers is broken and users cannot search for flights.
expectedOutcome: The flight search form is visible and usable on the landing page. Users can select origin and destination, pick a date, set passenger count, and submit to see search results.
hint: The form uses a library that shares state through React context. Ensure any component that consumes that context is rendered inside the correct provider.
solution: SearchFormFields calls useFormContext() from react-hook-form but is rendered without a FormProvider. useFormContext() throws when there is no provider, so the form subtree crashes and the UI shows an error fallback. Fix: wrap the form content that depends on context with FormProvider and pass the methods from useForm() (e.g. <FormProvider {...methods}><SearchFormFields /></FormProvider>).

playwrightTests.name: should display the flight search form on the landing page
playwrightTests.description: Fails when bug is present (form crashes); passes when FormProvider is added and form renders.
playwrightTests.expectation: Form with role=search and Search Flights button is visible.

playwrightTests.name: should submit valid search and navigate to search results
playwrightTests.description: Fills origin, destination, date, submits; expects redirect to /search and results or empty state.
playwrightTests.expectation: After submitting valid data, URL is /search and page shows search-related content.

playwrightTests.name: should show validation errors when submitting empty form
playwrightTests.description: Submit without filling required fields; form should show validation messages and not navigate.
playwrightTests.expectation: Validation errors appear, user remains on landing page.
